compiled_state.title: [test-cross-stack-regression]: pre-existing test failure on main branch
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description: tests/scripts/test-cross-stack-regression.sh is failing on the main branch (pre-existing, not introduced by recent changes). Confirmed pre-existing via git stash test. Needs investigation to determine root cause and fix.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC) — test failure in detect-stack.sh cross-stack regression suite. Severity: low (test-only failure, no production impact). Complexity: simple (single test file). Environment: local/CI. Intermittent: deterministic (consistent fail at ticket creation time).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: agent failure — inline fallback (sub-agent context). Test currently passing on 3 consecutive runs — treating as intent-aligned since test suite behavior aligns with intent (cross-stack regression tests should pass).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Step 2-3 Investigation (inline fallback): ROOT_CAUSE confirmed. The test test_cross_stack_multi_marker_python_priority referenced a wrong fixture path: tests/evals/fixtures/multi-marker-project instead of tests/fixtures/multi-marker-project. The path does not exist, so the guard branch assert_eq fires 'exists' vs 'missing' causing a FAILED assertion. Additionally, the fixture tests/fixtures/multi-marker-project was not yet created. Both issues were fixed in commit 1f5e79616d (2026-04-25) which: (1) corrected the path reference in the test, (2) created the fixture directory with pyproject.toml and package.json. Hypothesis tested empirically: tests/evals/fixtures/multi-marker-project does not exist (confirmed), tests/fixtures/multi-marker-project exists (confirmed). Test now passes 3/3 consecutive runs. RESOLUTION: already fixed by commit 1f5e79616d.
comments.author: Test
